stub: She is a Labour MP who comes from a white working class background.
constituency: She is from a constituency that is not your constituency.
personalization: She tends to vote in accordance with the instructions of her party, even when that conflicts with her own beliefs.
substantive: She opposes increasing the basic Universal Credit grant by £20 per week.
justification: In political debates, she regularly uses arguments that appeal to society as a whole.
responsiveness: Asked recently about the role and duties of politicians, she said:  “Elections are important because they force me as a politician to listen and respond to the views of voters.”
text: She is a Labour MP who comes from a white working class background. She is from a constituency that is not your constituency. She tends to vote in accordance with the instructions of her party, even when that conflicts with her own beliefs. She opposes increasing the basic Universal Credit grant by £20 per week. In political debates, she regularly uses arguments that appeal to society as a whole. Asked recently about the role and duties of politicians, she said:  “Elections are important because they force me as a politician to listen and respond to the views of voters.”
